The original test case uses identical shapes [1,10,10] for all tensors, but baddbmm requires valid batch matrix multiplication: x @ y must be defined — i.e., x.shape[-1] == y.shape[-2]. Here x=[1,10,10], y=[1,10,10] implies x@y is [1,10,10], which is valid, but inconsistency suggests numerical divergence due to uninitialized values. To ensure deterministic comparison, inputs must be controlled (e.g., constant or random seed). However, the root cause is parameter mapping mismatch in test construction: PyTorch's batch1=batch2=[1,10,10] is fine, but Paddle's x and y must match batch1/batch2 semantics exactly.
